en.description: Orion is one of the most recognizable constellations in the night sky, visible from virtually everywhere on Earth. It depicts a hunter standing with a raised club and shield, dominated by the three-star belt — Alnitak, Alnilam, and Mintaka — and bordered by the red supergiant Betelgeuse (upper left shoulder) and the blue supergiant Rigel (lower right foot). The constellation contains some of the most spectacular deep-sky objects, including the Orion Nebula (M42), a stellar nursery visible to the naked eye as a fuzzy patch below the belt. Orion is best seen in January and February and contains more first-magnitude stars than any other constellation.
en.mythology: In Greek mythology, Orion was the greatest hunter who ever lived — son of the sea god Poseidon and the mortal Euryale. Blessed with godlike strength and beauty, he boasted he could slay every animal on Earth, enraging the goddess Gaia. She sent a giant scorpion to kill him, and the two fought so fiercely that Zeus placed both in the sky on opposite sides so they would never meet again. Another version says Orion was killed by Artemis, goddess of the hunt, either in jealousy or by Apollo's trickery. Orion eternally pursues the Pleiades sisters across the sky. The Orion Nebula within the constellation was known to ancient peoples worldwide — the Maya saw it as a cosmic hearth where gods created the first humans.

id.description: Orion adalah salah satu konstelasi paling mudah dikenali di langit malam, terlihat dari hampir seluruh penjuru Bumi. Konstelasi ini menggambarkan seorang pemburu berdiri dengan gada terangkat dan perisai, didominasi oleh sabuk tiga bintang — Alnitak, Alnilam, dan Mintaka — dengan supergiant merah Betelgeuse di bahu kiri atas dan supergiant biru Rigel di kaki kanan bawah. Konstelasi ini mengandung beberapa objek langit dalam paling spektakuler, termasuk Nebula Orion (M42), sebuah tempat lahirnya bintang yang terlihat mata telanjang sebagai bercak kabur di bawah sabuk. Orion paling baik diamati pada Januari dan Februari.
id.mythology: Dalam mitologi Yunani, Orion adalah pemburu terhebat yang pernah hidup — putra dewa laut Poseidon dan manusia fana Euryale. Diberkahi kekuatan dan keindahan seperti dewa, ia menyombongkan diri bisa membunuh setiap hewan di Bumi, membuat murka dewi Gaia. Gaia mengirim kalajengking raksasa untuk membunuhnya, dan keduanya bertarung begitu sengit sehingga Zeus menempatkan keduanya di langit di sisi yang berlawanan agar tidak pernah bertemu lagi. Orion selamanya mengejar saudari Pleiades melintasi langit.

en.description: Scorpius is one of the most spectacular constellations in the night sky, resembling a scorpion with a curved tail ending in the stinger stars Shaula and Lesath. Its heart is marked by the brilliant red supergiant Antares, one of the largest stars visible to the naked eye. Scorpius is best viewed from the southern hemisphere and tropical latitudes, where it rises high in the sky. The region around Scorpius is one of the richest in the Milky Way, containing numerous star clusters and nebulae. It is one of the few constellations that genuinely resembles the figure it represents.
en.mythology: In Greek mythology, Scorpius was the great scorpion sent by the goddess Gaia (or Artemis, in some versions) to kill the boastful hunter Orion, who had claimed he could slay every creature on Earth. The scorpion succeeded in killing Orion, and Zeus immortalized both in the sky — but placed them on opposite sides so they would never meet. This is why Orion sets in the west as Scorpius rises in the east. In Babylonian astronomy, the scorpion constellation (GIR.TAB) was one of the oldest constellations, associated with the scorpion-men who guarded the gates of the underworld and protected the sun god Shamash on his nightly passage. The Maori of New Zealand saw Scorpius as the fishhook of the demigod Maui, used to fish up the North Island from the ocean.

id.description: Scorpius adalah salah satu konstelasi paling spektakuler di langit malam, menyerupai kalajengking dengan ekor melengkung yang berujung pada bintang-bintang sengat Shaula dan Lesath. Jantungnya ditandai oleh supergiant merah yang cemerlang, Antares, salah satu bintang terbesar yang terlihat mata telanjang. Scorpius paling baik diamati dari belahan bumi selatan dan lintang tropis. Wilayah sekitar Scorpius adalah salah satu yang paling kaya di Bima Sakti, mengandung banyak gugus bintang dan nebula.
id.mythology: Dalam mitologi Yunani, Scorpius adalah kalajengking agung yang dikirim oleh dewi Gaia untuk membunuh pemburu sombong Orion, yang mengklaim bisa membunuh setiap makhluk di Bumi. Kalajengking berhasil membunuh Orion, dan Zeus mengabadikan keduanya di langit — tetapi menempatkan mereka di sisi yang berlawanan agar tidak pernah bertemu. Inilah mengapa Orion terbenam di barat saat Scorpius terbit di timur. Dalam astronomi Babilonia, konstelasi kalajengking (GIR.TAB) adalah salah satu konstelasi tertua, dikaitkan dengan manusia kalajengking yang menjaga gerbang dunia bawah.

en.description: Lyra is a small but brilliant constellation representing the lyre, the ancient musical instrument made from a tortoise shell. Its brightest star Vega is the fifth-brightest in the night sky and one of the vertices of the Summer Triangle. Despite its small size, Lyra contains the famous Ring Nebula (M57), one of the best examples of a planetary nebula — the glowing shell of gas expelled by a dying star. Lyra is best viewed in summer from the northern hemisphere, when Vega shines nearly overhead. The constellation forms a distinctive small parallelogram of stars below Vega.
en.mythology: Lyra represents the lyre of Orpheus, the legendary musician of Greek mythology whose music was so beautiful it could charm wild animals, make rivers flow uphill, and cause rocks to weep. Apollo gave Orpheus the lyre and he became the greatest musician in the world. When his beloved wife Eurydice died from a snake bite, Orpheus descended into Hades and played his lyre so movingly that even the god of the dead wept and agreed to release Eurydice — on the condition Orpheus not look back as he led her out. He turned back at the last moment out of love and anxiety, losing her forever. After Orpheus was killed by the Maenads, the Muses gathered the pieces of his lyre and placed it in the sky.

id.description: Lyra adalah konstelasi kecil namun cemerlang yang mewakili kecapi, instrumen musik kuno yang terbuat dari cangkang kura-kura. Bintang paling terangnya, Vega, adalah kelima paling terang di langit malam dan salah satu titik sudut Segitiga Musim Panas. Meski kecil, Lyra mengandung Nebula Cincin (M57) yang terkenal — cangkang gas bercahaya yang dikeluarkan oleh bintang yang sekarat. Lyra paling baik diamati di musim panas dari belahan bumi utara.
id.mythology: Lyra mewakili kecapi Orpheus, musisi legendaris dalam mitologi Yunani yang musiknya begitu indah sehingga bisa memikat binatang buas, membuat sungai mengalir ke atas, dan menyebabkan batu menangis. Apollo memberikan kecapi kepada Orpheus dan ia menjadi musisi terhebat di dunia. Ketika istri tercintanya Eurydice meninggal digigit ular, Orpheus turun ke Hades dan memainkan kecapinya begitu memilukan sehingga bahkan dewa orang mati pun menangis. Setelah Orpheus dibunuh oleh para Maenad, para Muse menempatkan kecapinya di langit.

en.description: Canis Major is the Greater Dog constellation, dominated by Sirius — the brightest star in the entire night sky. It represents one of the two dogs following Orion the Hunter across the sky. Despite its modest size, Canis Major contains several other notable stars including Adhara, Wezen, and Mirzam. The constellation is best viewed in February from the northern hemisphere. Sirius's brightness and its appearance in the summer sky led to the phrase 'dog days of summer', as the ancient Greeks believed Sirius's combined heat with the Sun caused the hottest days of summer.
en.mythology: In Greek mythology, Canis Major represents Laelaps, the magical dog that was destined to catch any prey it pursued. Zeus originally gave this dog to Europa as a gift, and it eventually came into the possession of the Theban hero Cephalus. The dog was set to catch the Teumessian Fox, a creature destined never to be caught — a paradox that Zeus resolved by turning both animals to stone and placing them in the sky. In another tradition, Canis Major is the faithful hunting hound of Orion, following the great hunter across the celestial sphere. To the ancient Egyptians, Sirius (in Canis Major) was the most important star in the sky, rising just before the annual flooding of the Nile and marking the new year.

id.description: Canis Major adalah konstelasi Anjing Besar, didominasi oleh Sirius — bintang paling terang di seluruh langit malam. Konstelasi ini mewakili salah satu dari dua anjing yang mengikuti Orion sang Pemburu melintasi langit. Meski ukurannya sedang, Canis Major mengandung beberapa bintang notable lainnya termasuk Adhara, Wezen, dan Mirzam. Kecemerlangan Sirius dan kemunculannya di langit musim panas menyebabkan ungkapan 'hari-hari anjing musim panas'.
id.mythology: Dalam mitologi Yunani, Canis Major mewakili Laelaps, anjing ajaib yang ditakdirkan untuk menangkap mangsa apapun yang dikejarnya. Zeus awalnya memberikan anjing ini kepada Europa sebagai hadiah. Anjing itu diatur untuk mengejar Rubah Teumessian, makhluk yang ditakdirkan tidak pernah tertangkap — paradoks yang diselesaikan Zeus dengan mengubah kedua hewan menjadi batu dan menempatkan mereka di langit. Bagi orang Mesir kuno, Sirius di Canis Major adalah bintang terpenting di langit, menandai awal tahun baru.

en.description: Canis Minor is a small constellation containing just two main stars — Procyon, the eighth-brightest star in the night sky, and Gomeisa. Despite its modest size, Canis Minor is part of the Winter Triangle asterism along with Sirius and Betelgeuse. Procyon, located just 11.5 light-years away, is one of the closest stars to Earth visible to the naked eye. The constellation is best viewed in February from the northern hemisphere and has been recognized as a small dog since ancient times.
en.mythology: In Greek mythology, Canis Minor represents the smaller of the two dogs following Orion the Hunter across the sky. One tradition identifies it as the dog of the hunter Orion. Another version associates it with the dog Maera, who belonged to the Athenian Icarius — the mortal taught winemaking by Dionysus. When Icarius was killed by shepherds who thought his wine was poison, his faithful dog Maera led his daughter Erigone to the body. The gods immortalized the dog as Canis Minor as reward for this loyalty. Procyon's name meaning 'before the dog' — because it rises just before Sirius (the main Dog Star) — reflects its role as herald of Canis Major.

id.description: Canis Minor adalah konstelasi kecil yang hanya memiliki dua bintang utama — Procyon, bintang kedelapan paling terang di langit malam, dan Gomeisa. Meski ukurannya sederhana, Canis Minor adalah bagian dari asterisma Segitiga Musim Dingin bersama Sirius dan Betelgeuse. Procyon, hanya 11,5 tahun cahaya jauhnya, adalah salah satu bintang terdekat dari Bumi yang terlihat mata telanjang.
id.mythology: Dalam mitologi Yunani, Canis Minor mewakili yang lebih kecil dari dua anjing yang mengikuti Orion sang Pemburu melintasi langit. Satu tradisi mengidentifikasinya sebagai anjing Icarius — manusia fana yang diajarkan pembuatan anggur oleh Dionysus. Ketika Icarius dibunuh oleh para gembala, anjing setianya Maera memimpin putri Icarius ke jasadnya. Para dewa mengabadikan anjing itu sebagai Canis Minor sebagai penghargaan atas kesetiaan ini.

en.description: Aquila the Eagle is a distinctive constellation on the celestial equator, best known for its brightest star Altair — one of the three stars forming the Summer Triangle. The constellation's three main stars Tarazed, Altair, and Alshain form a recognizable straight line representing the eagle's body and wings. Altair is only 16.7 light-years from Earth, making it one of the closest bright stars. The Milky Way passes through Aquila, making the region rich in star clusters and nebulae. Aquila is best viewed in summer from the northern hemisphere.
en.mythology: In Greek mythology, Aquila represents the eagle of Zeus, the mightiest of all birds and the king of the gods' divine servant. The eagle carried Zeus's thunderbolts across the sky and executed his will among mortals and immortals alike. In one famous myth, Aquila was sent by Zeus to abduct the beautiful Trojan youth Ganymede, the most handsome mortal alive, to serve as cupbearer to the gods on Olympus. In another tradition, Aquila was the eagle that daily tore out the liver of the Titan Prometheus, condemned to eternal punishment for stealing fire from the gods. In East Asian astronomy, Altair (the eagle's central star) represented Niulang the Cowherd in the beloved Qixi love story, separated from Vega (the Weaver Girl) by the Milky Way.

id.description: Aquila sang Elang adalah konstelasi khas di ekuator langit, paling dikenal dengan bintang paling terangnya, Altair — salah satu dari tiga bintang yang membentuk Segitiga Musim Panas. Tiga bintang utama konstelasi, Tarazed, Altair, dan Alshain, membentuk garis lurus yang dapat dikenali mewakili tubuh dan sayap elang. Altair hanya 16,7 tahun cahaya dari Bumi, menjadikannya salah satu bintang terang terdekat.
id.mythology: Dalam mitologi Yunani, Aquila mewakili elang Zeus, yang perkasa dari semua burung dan pelayan ilahi raja para dewa. Elang membawa petir Zeus melintasi langit. Dalam mitos terkenal, Aquila dikirim Zeus untuk menculik pemuda Troya tampan Ganymede untuk melayani sebagai penuang air bagi para dewa di Olimpus. Dalam tradisi lain, Aquila adalah elang yang setiap hari mencabut hati Titan Prometheus yang dikutuk karena mencuri api dari para dewa.

en.description: Boötes the Herdsman is a large northern constellation dominated by Arcturus, the brightest star in the northern hemisphere and fourth-brightest in the night sky. The constellation resembles a kite or ice cream cone shape and is best seen in spring and summer. Boötes contains the Arcturus stream — a group of stars moving together through space that may originate from an ancient satellite galaxy absorbed by the Milky Way. The name Boötes may derive from Greek meaning 'herdsman' or 'ox-driver', referring to his role driving the oxen of the plow stars Ursa Major.
en.mythology: In Greek mythology, Boötes has several origin stories. One tradition identifies him with Arcas, son of Zeus and the nymph Callisto. Callisto was transformed into a bear by the jealous Hera, and when her son Arcas nearly killed her while hunting, Zeus transformed Arcas into a smaller bear and placed both in the sky as Ursa Major and Ursa Minor, with Boötes as the guardian. Another tradition says Boötes was the son of Demeter and the first man to use a plow, teaching agriculture to humanity. He was rewarded with a place in the stars for this gift to civilization. The bright star Arcturus ('Guardian of the Bear') reflects Boötes's eternal role watching over the celestial bears.

id.description: Boötes sang Gembala adalah konstelasi utara yang besar didominasi oleh Arcturus, bintang paling terang di belahan bumi utara dan keempat paling terang di langit malam. Konstelasi ini menyerupai bentuk layang-layang dan paling baik terlihat di musim semi dan panas. Nama Boötes mungkin berasal dari bahasa Yunani yang berarti 'gembala' atau 'pengemudi lembu', merujuk pada perannya mengemudi lembu bajak bintang-bintang Ursa Major.
id.mythology: Dalam mitologi Yunani, Boötes memiliki beberapa cerita asal. Satu tradisi mengidentifikasinya dengan Arcas, putra Zeus dan nimfa Callisto. Callisto diubah menjadi beruang oleh Hera yang cemburu, dan ketika putranya Arcas hampir membunuhnya saat berburu, Zeus mengubah Arcas menjadi beruang lebih kecil dan menempatkan keduanya di langit. Tradisi lain mengatakan Boötes adalah orang pertama yang menggunakan bajak, mengajarkan pertanian kepada umat manusia.

en.description: Taurus the Bull is one of the oldest and most prominent zodiac constellations, featuring the brilliant orange giant Aldebaran as the eye of the bull. The constellation contains two famous star clusters — the Pleiades (Seven Sisters) and the Hyades (the V-shaped face of the bull), both visible to the naked eye. The Crab Nebula (M1), the remnant of a supernova explosion observed in 1054 CE, also lies within Taurus. The constellation is best viewed in January from the northern hemisphere. Taurus was one of the first constellations identified, with cave paintings at Lascaux in France dating to 17,000 BCE possibly depicting Taurus and the Pleiades.
en.mythology: In Greek mythology, Taurus represents the bull form Zeus took when he fell in love with the Phoenician princess Europa. Zeus transformed into a beautiful white bull and mingled with her father's herd. Europa was enchanted by the gentle bull and climbed on its back, whereupon Zeus galloped to the sea and swam to Crete, where he revealed his true identity. Europa became the mother of King Minos of Crete, whose palace contained the labyrinth housing the Minotaur — a being with a bull's head. In Babylonian astronomy, Taurus was the Bull of Heaven (GUD.AN.NA), one of the most important constellations, associated with the god Anu and the spring equinox. The Pleiades within Taurus were the 'Seven Sisters' whose rising marked the beginning of the agricultural season.

id.description: Taurus sang Banteng adalah salah satu konstelasi zodiak tertua dan paling menonjol, menampilkan raksasa oranye cemerlang Aldebaran sebagai mata banteng. Konstelasi ini mengandung dua gugus bintang terkenal — Pleiades (Tujuh Saudari) dan Hyades, keduanya terlihat mata telanjang. Nebula Kepiting (M1), sisa ledakan supernova yang diamati pada 1054 M, juga terletak di dalam Taurus. Lukisan gua di Lascaux, Prancis, bertanggal 17.000 SM mungkin menggambarkan Taurus dan Pleiades.
id.mythology: Dalam mitologi Yunani, Taurus mewakili bentuk banteng yang diambil Zeus ketika ia jatuh cinta dengan putri Fenisia Europa. Zeus berubah menjadi banteng putih yang indah dan bergabung dengan kawanan ayah Europa. Setelah Europa naik ke punggungnya, Zeus berlari ke laut dan berenang ke Kreta. Dalam astronomi Babilonia, Taurus adalah Banteng Surga (GUD.AN.NA), salah satu konstelasi terpenting, dikaitkan dengan dewa Anu dan titik balik matahari musim semi.

en.description: Carina the Keel is a southern constellation containing Canopus — the second-brightest star in the night sky. It was originally part of the enormous ancient constellation Argo Navis, representing the ship of Jason and the Argonauts, which the International Astronomical Union divided into three smaller constellations in 1930: Carina (keel), Puppis (stern), and Vela (sails). Carina also contains the spectacular Eta Carinae Nebula (NGC 3372), one of the largest and most luminous nebulae in the Milky Way, surrounding the hypergiant star Eta Carinae which may soon explode as a supernova.
en.mythology: Carina was historically part of Argo Navis — the ship of Jason and the fifty Argonauts in their legendary quest for the Golden Fleece. The Argo was said to be the first ship ever built, constructed by the master craftsman Argus under Athena's guidance, incorporating a magical speaking plank from the sacred oak grove of Dodona. The ship sailed from Iolcos through the Clashing Rocks (Symplegades), past the land of the Amazons, to Colchis at the far end of the Black Sea. After Jason obtained the Golden Fleece with help from the sorceress Medea, the gods honored the Argo by placing it in the sky. The constellation was so large it was eventually divided into three parts to make celestial navigation more practical.

id.description: Carina sang Lunas adalah konstelasi selatan yang mengandung Canopus — bintang paling terang kedua di langit malam. Dulunya bagian dari konstelasi besar kuno Argo Navis, yang dibagi IAU menjadi tiga konstelasi lebih kecil pada 1930: Carina (lunas), Puppis (buritan), dan Vela (layar). Carina juga mengandung Nebula Eta Carinae yang spektakuler, salah satu nebula terbesar dan paling terang di Bima Sakti.
id.mythology: Carina secara historis merupakan bagian dari Argo Navis — kapal Jason dan lima puluh Argonaut dalam pencarian Bulu Domba Emas yang legendaris. Argo dikatakan sebagai kapal pertama yang pernah dibangun, dikonstruksi oleh pengrajin Argus di bawah bimbingan Athena. Kapal berlayar dari Iolcos melalui Bebatuan yang Bertubrukan, melewati negeri Amazon, ke Kolkhis di ujung jauh Laut Hitam. Setelah Jason mendapatkan Bulu Domba Emas dengan bantuan penyihir Medea, para dewa menghormati Argo dengan menempatkannya di langit.

en.description: Aries the Ram is the first sign of the zodiac, marking the point where the Sun crosses the celestial equator at the vernal equinox — though due to precession, this point has since moved into Pisces. Aries is a relatively faint constellation with no stars brighter than magnitude 2, but its historical importance as the 'first point of Aries' made it one of the most significant constellations in ancient astronomy. Its brightest stars form a distinctive curved line representing the ram's horns. Aries is best viewed in November and December.
en.mythology: In Greek mythology, Aries represents the ram with the golden fleece, sent by the god Hermes to rescue the children Phrixus and Helle from their cruel stepmother Ino. The ram flew the children through the air — but Helle fell into the sea (naming the Hellespont, modern Dardanelles). Phrixus safely reached Colchis, where he sacrificed the ram to Zeus and hung its golden fleece in the sacred grove of Ares, guarded by a sleepless dragon. This Golden Fleece later became the object of Jason's famous quest with the Argonauts. Zeus immortalized the ram's sacrifice by placing it in the sky. In Babylonian astronomy, Aries was associated with the god Dumuzi and marked the beginning of the agricultural year.

id.description: Aries sang Domba Jantan adalah tanda zodiak pertama, menandai titik di mana Matahari melintasi ekuator langit pada ekuinoks vernal — meski karena presesi, titik ini telah bergeser ke Pisces. Aries adalah konstelasi yang relatif redup, tidak ada bintang yang lebih terang dari magnitudo 2, tetapi kepentingan historisnya sebagai 'titik pertama Aries' menjadikannya salah satu konstelasi terpenting dalam astronomi kuno.
id.mythology: Dalam mitologi Yunani, Aries mewakili domba jantan berbulu emas, dikirim oleh dewa Hermes untuk menyelamatkan anak-anak Phrixus dan Helle dari ibu tiri mereka yang kejam Ino. Domba itu membawa anak-anak terbang di udara — tetapi Helle jatuh ke laut (menamai Hellespont). Phrixus selamat mencapai Kolkhis, di mana ia mengorbankan domba dan menggantung bulunya di hutan suci Ares, dijaga oleh naga yang tidak pernah tidur. Bulu Domba Emas ini kemudian menjadi tujuan pencarian Jason yang terkenal.

en.description: Gemini the Twins is a zodiac constellation marked by two parallel lines of stars representing the legendary twins Castor and Pollux — its two brightest stars. The constellation is best viewed in February from the northern hemisphere. Gemini contains the Eskimo Nebula (NGC 2392), a planetary nebula with a bright central star surrounded by a faint outer halo. The Geminid meteor shower, one of the most prolific annual meteor showers, appears to radiate from Gemini each December. Castor, despite its alpha designation, is actually slightly fainter than Pollux.
en.mythology: Gemini represents Castor and Pollux, the Divine Twins (Dioscuri) of Greek and Roman mythology — sons of Queen Leda of Sparta. The twins had different fathers: Castor was the mortal son of King Tyndareus, while Pollux was the immortal son of Zeus, who visited Leda in the form of a swan. Despite their different natures, the brothers were inseparable — renowned as skilled horsemen, warriors, and protectors of sailors. When Castor was killed in battle, the immortal Pollux was so grief-stricken he asked Zeus to share his immortality. Zeus allowed them to alternate between Olympus and Hades, and placed both together in the sky. The Romans venerated Castor and Pollux as the Dioscuri, who famously appeared on white horses to aid Roman armies in battle.

id.description: Gemini sang Kembar adalah konstelasi zodiak yang ditandai oleh dua garis bintang paralel yang mewakili si kembar legendaris Castor dan Pollux — dua bintang paling terangnya. Konstelasi ini paling baik dilihat pada Februari dari belahan bumi utara. Gemini mengandung Nebula Eskimo (NGC 2392), sebuah nebula planetari. Hujan meteor Geminid, salah satu hujan meteor tahunan paling produktif, tampak memancar dari Gemini setiap Desember.
id.mythology: Gemini mewakili Castor dan Pollux, Si Kembar Ilahi (Dioscuri) dalam mitologi Yunani dan Romawi — putra Ratu Leda dari Sparta. Kembar itu memiliki ayah yang berbeda: Castor adalah putra manusia fana Raja Tyndareus, sementara Pollux adalah putra abadi Zeus. Meski berbeda sifat, saudara itu tidak terpisahkan — terkenal sebagai penunggang kuda terampil, pejuang, dan pelindung para pelaut. Ketika Castor tewas dalam pertempuran, Zeus menempatkan keduanya bersama di langit.

en.description: Cancer the Crab is the faintest of the twelve zodiac constellations, with no stars brighter than magnitude 3.5. Despite its dimness, Cancer contains the Beehive Cluster (Praesepe or M44), one of the nearest open star clusters to Earth, visible to the naked eye as a misty patch. The cluster contains over 1,000 stars. Cancer also marks the position of the Sun during the summer solstice in ancient times — giving its name to the Tropic of Cancer. Cancer is best viewed in March from the northern hemisphere.
en.mythology: In Greek mythology, Cancer represents the crab that pinched the hero Hercules during his battle with the Lernaean Hydra — the second of his twelve labors. The giant water serpent regrew two heads for every one Hercules cut off, and while he was fighting it, the goddess Hera (who hated Hercules) sent a crab to distract him by pinching his foot. Hercules simply stepped on the crab and crushed it. Despite its brief and unheroic role, Hera honored the crab's loyalty by placing it in the sky as the constellation Cancer. In Babylonian astronomy, Cancer was associated with the eastern gate of the sun — the point where the sun entered the sky at summer solstice — giving it great cosmological significance despite its faint appearance.

id.description: Cancer sang Kepiting adalah yang paling redup dari dua belas konstelasi zodiak, tidak ada bintang yang lebih terang dari magnitudo 3,5. Meski redup, Cancer mengandung Gugus Sarang Lebah (Praesepe atau M44), salah satu gugus bintang terbuka terdekat dari Bumi, terlihat mata telanjang sebagai bercak berkabut. Cancer juga menandai posisi Matahari selama titik balik matahari musim panas di zaman kuno — memberikan namanya pada Garis Balik Utara (Tropic of Cancer).
id.mythology: Dalam mitologi Yunani, Cancer mewakili kepiting yang mencubit pahlawan Hercules selama pertempurannya dengan Hydra Lernaean — tugas keduanya dari dua belas tugas. Sementara Hercules bertarung melawan ular air raksasa, dewi Hera mengirim kepiting untuk mengalihkan perhatiannya dengan mencubit kakinya. Hercules langsung menginjak kepiting dan menghancurkannya. Meski perannya singkat dan tidak heroik, Hera menghormati kesetiaan kepiting dengan menempatkannya di langit sebagai konstelasi Cancer.

en.description: Leo the Lion is one of the most recognizable zodiac constellations, with a distinctive shape resembling a crouching lion. The bright star Regulus marks the lion's heart, while the curved 'sickle' or 'backward question mark' asterism forms the head and mane. Denebola marks the tail. Leo contains several bright galaxies including the Leo Triplet (M65, M66, NGC 3628) and the Leo I dwarf galaxy. The Leonid meteor shower, which can produce spectacular storms, appears to radiate from Leo each November. Leo is best viewed in April from the northern hemisphere.
en.mythology: In Greek mythology, Leo represents the Nemean Lion, the monstrous first labor of Hercules. The lion terrorized the region of Nemea with an impenetrable golden hide — no weapon could pierce it. Hercules was tasked with killing it as punishment for a crime. After his arrows and sword failed, Hercules wrestled the lion with his bare hands and strangled it. He used the lion's own razor-sharp claws to skin it, and wore the invulnerable hide as armor for the rest of his adventures. Zeus immortalized the great beast by placing it in the sky as Leo. In ancient Egypt, Leo's stars were associated with the annual flooding of the Nile — the sun entered Leo during the hottest part of summer when the floods began. The sphinx was said to be Leo-bodied with a human face.

id.description: Leo sang Singa adalah salah satu konstelasi zodiak paling mudah dikenali, dengan bentuk khas menyerupai singa yang merangkak. Bintang terang Regulus menandai jantung singa, sementara asterisma 'arit' atau 'tanda tanya terbalik' yang melengkung membentuk kepala dan surai. Leo mengandung beberapa galaksi terang termasuk Leo Triplet (M65, M66, NGC 3628). Hujan meteor Leonid, yang dapat menghasilkan badai spektakuler, tampak memancar dari Leo setiap November.
id.mythology: Dalam mitologi Yunani, Leo mewakili Singa Nemean, tugas pertama yang mengerikan dari Hercules. Singa itu meneror wilayah Nemea dengan kulit emas yang tidak bisa ditembus — tidak ada senjata yang bisa menembusnya. Hercules bergulat dengan singa menggunakan tangan kosong dan mencekiknya. Ia menggunakan cakar tajam singa sendiri untuk menguliti, dan mengenakan kulit yang tidak bisa ditembus sebagai baju besi untuk sisa petualangannya. Zeus mengabadikan binatang agung itu di langit sebagai Leo.

en.description: Virgo the Maiden is the largest zodiac constellation and second-largest constellation overall, spanning a vast area of sky. Its brightest star Spica forms part of the Spring Triangle with Arcturus and Denebola. Virgo is famous for the Virgo Cluster — a massive supercluster of over 1,300 galaxies including M87, home to a supermassive black hole imaged for the first time in 2019. The Virgo Cluster is the heart of the Local Supercluster. Virgo is best viewed in May from the northern hemisphere and contains more Messier objects than any other constellation.
en.mythology: Virgo is most commonly associated with Demeter (Greek) or Ceres (Roman), goddess of grain, agriculture, and the harvest. Virgo holds a sheaf of wheat, represented by the star Spica. In the myth, Demeter's daughter Persephone was abducted by Hades and taken to the underworld. Demeter's grief caused crops to wither and winter to descend on the earth. Zeus eventually negotiated Persephone's partial return — she spends half the year above ground (spring and summer, when Virgo is prominent in the sky) and half below (autumn and winter, when Virgo disappears). Another tradition identifies Virgo with Astraea, the last of the gods to leave Earth as the Golden Age ended, who later became the constellation Virgo with her scales of justice becoming Libra. The star Spica was used by Hipparchus to discover the precession of the equinoxes.

id.description: Virgo sang Gadis adalah konstelasi zodiak terbesar dan konstelasi terbesar kedua secara keseluruhan, mencakup area langit yang luas. Bintang paling terangnya, Spica, membentuk bagian dari Segitiga Musim Semi bersama Arcturus dan Denebola. Virgo terkenal dengan Gugus Virgo — supercluster masif yang terdiri dari lebih dari 1.300 galaksi termasuk M87, rumah bagi lubang hitam supermasif yang pertama kali dicitrakan pada 2019. Virgo paling baik dilihat pada Mei dari belahan bumi utara.
id.mythology: Virgo paling umum dikaitkan dengan Demeter (Yunani) atau Ceres (Romawi), dewi gandum, pertanian, dan panen. Virgo memegang seikat gandum, diwakili oleh bintang Spica. Dalam mitologi, putri Demeter, Persephone, diculik oleh Hades dan dibawa ke dunia bawah. Kesedihan Demeter menyebabkan tanaman layu dan musim dingin melanda bumi. Zeus bernegosiasi untuk kepulangan sebagian Persephone — ia menghabiskan setengah tahun di atas tanah (musim semi dan panas, ketika Virgo menonjol di langit) dan setengahnya di bawah.

en.description: Libra the Scales is the only zodiac constellation representing an inanimate object. It is a moderately faint constellation between Virgo and Scorpius, best viewed in June from the northern hemisphere. Libra contains the star Zubeneschamali, which some observers describe as having a slightly greenish tint — one of the very few stars ever described as appearing green to the naked eye. The constellation also contains Gliese 581, a red dwarf star with a planetary system that once attracted attention as a potential host for habitable planets.
en.mythology: Libra was originally the claws of Scorpius in ancient Babylonian and Greek astronomy — the transition from scorpion claws to scales reflects a cultural shift in ancient Rome toward themes of justice and balance. The Romans created Libra as a constellation honoring Julius Caesar and Roman law. Libra was associated with Astraea, goddess of justice, the last of the immortals to live on Earth during the Golden Age before ascending to the sky as Virgo — with her scales of justice becoming Libra. The constellation marked the autumnal equinox in ancient times, when day and night were perfectly balanced — making the scales symbolism especially apt. In some traditions, Libra represents the scales held by Themis or Dike, the goddesses of divine and human justice respectively.

id.description: Libra sang Timbangan adalah satu-satunya konstelasi zodiak yang mewakili benda mati. Ini adalah konstelasi yang cukup redup antara Virgo dan Scorpius, paling baik dilihat pada Juni dari belahan bumi utara. Libra mengandung bintang Zubeneschamali, yang beberapa pengamat gambarkan memiliki rona kehijauan — salah satu dari sangat sedikit bintang yang pernah digambarkan tampak hijau bagi mata telanjang.
id.mythology: Libra awalnya adalah cakar Scorpius dalam astronomi Babilonia dan Yunani kuno — transisi dari cakar kalajengking ke timbangan mencerminkan pergeseran budaya di Roma kuno menuju tema keadilan dan keseimbangan. Libra dikaitkan dengan Astraea, dewi keadilan, yang terakhir dari para abadi hidup di Bumi selama Zaman Keemasan sebelum naik ke langit sebagai Virgo — dengan timbangannya menjadi Libra. Konstelasi ini menandai ekuinoks vernal di zaman kuno, ketika siang dan malam seimbang sempurna.

en.description: Sagittarius the Archer is a rich zodiac constellation pointing its arrow toward the center of the Milky Way galaxy. The brightest stars form a recognizable 'Teapot' asterism, with the steam from the spout appearing as the dense star fields of the galactic center. The region is extraordinarily rich in deep-sky objects including the Lagoon Nebula (M8), Trifid Nebula (M20), Omega Nebula (M17), and numerous star clusters. Sagittarius contains more Messier objects than any other constellation. The supermassive black hole Sagittarius A* at the galaxy's center lies within this constellation.
en.mythology: Sagittarius's mythological identity has two competing versions in Greek tradition. In one, it represents Crotus, son of Pan the goat-god and a nymph, who lived on Mount Helicon among the Muses. He was a skilled archer and hunter who invented the art of archery and the practice of rhythmic clapping to show appreciation for music. The Muses asked Zeus to honor him in the sky. In another version, Sagittarius is the centaur Chiron — the wise and gentle half-man, half-horse who tutored heroes including Achilles, Jason, Asclepius, and Heracles. In Babylonian astronomy, Sagittarius was depicted as the god Pabilsag — a two-faced composite deity with a human torso, horse body, scorpion tail, and eagle wings, associated with war and the underworld transition.

id.description: Sagittarius sang Pemanah adalah konstelasi zodiak yang kaya mengarahkan panahnya ke pusat galaksi Bima Sakti. Bintang-bintang paling terang membentuk asterisma 'Teapot' yang mudah dikenali, dengan uap dari cerat tampak sebagai medan bintang yang padat dari pusat galaksi. Wilayah ini sangat kaya objek langit dalam termasuk Nebula Lagoon (M8), Nebula Trifid (M20), dan Nebula Omega (M17). Lubang hitam supermasif Sagittarius A* di pusat galaksi terletak di konstelasi ini.
id.mythology: Identitas mitologis Sagittarius memiliki dua versi dalam tradisi Yunani. Dalam satu versi, ia mewakili Crotus, putra Pan sang dewa kambing, yang tinggal di Gunung Helicon bersama para Muse. Ia adalah pemanah terampil yang menemukan seni memanah dan kebiasaan bertepuk tangan untuk menghargai musik. Dalam versi lain, Sagittarius adalah centaur Chiron — setengah manusia setengah kuda yang bijak dan lembut yang mengajar para pahlawan termasuk Achilles, Jason, dan Asclepius.

en.description: Capricornus the Sea-Goat is one of the faintest zodiac constellations, representing a mythological creature with the head and body of a goat and the tail of a fish. Despite its faintness, it is one of the oldest recognized constellations, with origins traceable to ancient Babylonian astronomy. The constellation is best viewed in September from the northern hemisphere. Capricornus contains the globular cluster M30, a tightly packed sphere of ancient stars about 28,000 light-years away. The brightest star Deneb Algedi is an eclipsing binary system.
en.mythology: In Greek mythology, Capricornus represents the god Pan, who transformed himself into a goat-fish to escape the monster Typhon when the giants attacked Olympus. The gods fled to Egypt and disguised themselves as animals — Pan jumped into the Nile River to escape, intending to transform fully into a fish, but only partially transformed before Typhon approached. His upper body became a goat while his lower half became a fish, creating the chimeric sea-goat figure. Zeus found this amusing and immortalized Pan's panicked half-transformation in the sky. In Babylonian astronomy, this constellation was SUHUR.MAS — the goat-fish — associated with the god Enki/Ea, lord of the subterranean freshwater ocean, who was often depicted with a goat's head and a fish's tail.

id.description: Capricornus sang Kambing Laut adalah salah satu konstelasi zodiak paling redup, mewakili makhluk mitologis dengan kepala dan tubuh kambing serta ekor ikan. Meski redup, ini adalah salah satu konstelasi tertua yang dikenali, dengan asal-usul dapat ditelusuri ke astronomi Babilonia kuno. Capricornus mengandung gugus bola M30, sebuah bola bintang tua yang sangat padat sekitar 28.000 tahun cahaya jauhnya.
id.mythology: Dalam mitologi Yunani, Capricornus mewakili dewa Pan, yang mengubah dirinya menjadi kambing-ikan untuk melarikan diri dari monster Typhon ketika para raksasa menyerang Olimpus. Para dewa melarikan diri ke Mesir dan menyamar sebagai hewan — Pan melompat ke Sungai Nil untuk melarikan diri. Tubuh atasnya menjadi kambing sementara bagian bawahnya menjadi ikan, menciptakan sosok kambing laut yang khimerik. Zeus mengabadikan transformasi setengah panik Pan di langit. Dalam astronomi Babilonia, konstelasi ini adalah SUHUR.MAS — kambing-ikan — dikaitkan dengan dewa Enki/Ea.

en.description: Aquarius the Water Bearer is a large zodiac constellation depicting a figure pouring water from an urn, best viewed in October from the northern hemisphere. Despite its large size, Aquarius is relatively faint. It contains the Helix Nebula (NGC 7293), the nearest planetary nebula to Earth at about 650 light-years — nicknamed the 'Eye of God' for its appearance in photographs. Aquarius also contains the Saturn Nebula (NGC 7009). The Aquarid meteor showers (both Eta Aquarids in May and Delta Aquarids in July) radiate from this constellation.
en.mythology: In Greek mythology, Aquarius most commonly represents Ganymede, the most beautiful mortal alive — a young Trojan prince. Zeus, enchanted by Ganymede's beauty, disguised himself as an eagle (the constellation Aquila) and swooped down to carry Ganymede up to Olympus. There, Zeus made Ganymede the cupbearer to the gods, pouring nectar and ambrosia at divine feasts. Zeus also compensated Ganymede's father with a herd of divine horses. In Babylonian astronomy, Aquarius was the god Ea (Enki), lord of the primordial freshwater ocean beneath the earth, depicted pouring streams of water — the source of all rivers and rain. The annual flooding of the Tigris and Euphrates rivers coincided with the sun's passage through Aquarius, connecting the constellation with life-giving water.

id.description: Aquarius sang Pembawa Air adalah konstelasi zodiak besar yang menggambarkan sosok yang menuangkan air dari kendi, paling baik dilihat pada Oktober dari belahan bumi utara. Meski ukurannya besar, Aquarius cukup redup. Konstelasi ini mengandung Nebula Helix (NGC 7293), nebula planetari terdekat dari Bumi pada sekitar 650 tahun cahaya — dijuluki 'Mata Tuhan' karena penampilannya dalam foto. Hujan meteor Aquarid (Eta Aquarid pada Mei dan Delta Aquarid pada Juli) memancar dari konstelasi ini.
id.mythology: Dalam mitologi Yunani, Aquarius paling umum mewakili Ganymede, manusia fana paling tampan yang pernah hidup — seorang pangeran muda Troya. Zeus, terpesona oleh kecantikan Ganymede, menyamar sebagai elang dan menculiknya ke Olimpus. Di sana, Zeus menjadikan Ganymede penuang air bagi para dewa. Dalam astronomi Babilonia, Aquarius adalah dewa Ea (Enki), penguasa lautan air tawar primordial di bawah bumi, digambarkan menuangkan aliran air — sumber semua sungai dan hujan. Banjir tahunan sungai Tigris dan Efrat bertepatan dengan perjalanan Matahari melalui Aquarius.

en.description: Pisces is the twelfth and final zodiac constellation, representing two fish tied together by their tails.
en.mythology: In Greek mythology, Pisces represents Aphrodite and her son Eros, who transformed into fish and tied themselves together to escape the monster Typhon.

id.description: Pisces adalah konstelasi zodiak kedua belas dan terakhir, melambangkan dua ikan yang diikat bersama pada ekornya.
id.mythology: Dalam mitologi Yunani, Pisces melambangkan Aphrodite dan putranya Eros, yang berubah menjadi ikan dan mengikat diri bersama untuk melarikan diri dari monster Typhon.
